subject: Physics
title: Compton Scattering
description: A fast breakdown of Compton Scattering for builders.

hook.text: In 1923, Arthur Compton bounced X-rays off electrons and proved photons carry momentum like billiard balls.

buildup.text: He aimed X-rays at graphite. The scattered rays came out with a longer wavelength than they went in.

discovery.text: The photon transferred some momentum to the electron, losing energy. Its wavelength increased.

twist.text: This was impossible if light were only a wave. Only particles carry momentum and transfer it in collisions.

climax.text: Compton scattering clinched the photon concept. Light truly behaves as a particle with measurable momentum.

punchline.text: X-rays bounce off electrons like balls. Photons are real particles.

quiz.question: What did Compton scattering prove about photons?
options: They carry momentum and behave like particles
They have no mass or energy
